current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: No reliable K=128 anchor exists in memory yet. This round should spend one proxy run on an explicitly pinned mainline topk baseline so subsequent architecture work can target material FVU improvement rather than comparing against an implicit or drifting default.

No reliable K=128 anchor exists in memory yet. This round should spend one proxy run on an explicitly pinned mainline topk baseline so subsequent architecture work can target material FVU improvement rather than comparing against an implicit or drifting default. full

The K=128 topk full baseline is now anchored at FVU 0.1853. The next round should probe a more expressive encoder family at the same K rather than shrinking K or spending another round on local topk tuning.

action.hypothesis: Because the frontier is empty, the next highest-value move is to establish a trustworthy K=128 quality anchor with the simplest mainline architecture before rewarding smaller K or drawing conclusions about new families. Run a proxy topk baseline at K=128 with the default expansion and optimizer explicitly pinned so later rounds can measure real FVU gains against a stable reference.
action.summary: No reliable K=128 anchor exists in memory yet. This round should spend one proxy run on an explicitly pinned mainline topk baseline so subsequent architecture work can target material FVU improvement rather than comparing against an implicit or drifting default.
action.self_review: This is intentionally conservative, but the memory is empty and the primary objective depends on having a trustworthy K=128 reference. I avoided architecture edits because there is not yet enough evidence to justify a new family over first establishing the anchor.
notes_to_memory: Round 0 starts with empty frontier and no trustworthy K=128 anchor, so anchor establishment takes priority over lower-K comparisons.
Treat this run as the baseline reference point for future aggressive FVU-improvement bets.
next_hypotheses: If this anchor is stable, escalate immediately to a higher-capacity or more expressive encoder family that could materially beat its FVU at the same K=128.
If the anchor quality is already much better than expected, then test quality-first upgrades such as mixture/factorized/residual encoders before considering smaller K.

action.hypothesis: A soft mixture-of-experts encoder may beat the current K=128 topk anchor by giving the encoder more routing expressivity without the hard-capacity bottlenecks of grouped routing. Test the existing mixture_topk family at the same K=128 and expansion factor as a quality-first proxy comparison.
action.summary: The K=128 topk full baseline is now anchored at FVU 0.1853. The next round should probe a more expressive encoder family at the same K rather than shrinking K or spending another round on local topk tuning.
action.self_review: This is the right point to open architecture exploration, but it is still a first incubation pass, so the result may be noisy or need follow-up tuning before judging the family. I kept the rest of the setup close to the anchor to preserve interpretability.
notes_to_memory: Established full K=128 topk anchor is FVU 0.18532327562570572; future lower-K or cost-driven moves should clear that quality bar first.
Round 2 opens the first new architecture family by testing mixture_topk as a quality-first encoder at the same K=128 anchor point.
next_hypotheses: If mixture_topk shows even modest proxy FVU gain, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before judging promotion.
If mixture_topk is flat or worse under normal runtime, try another expressive family such as residual_topk or factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor.

mixture_topk.design_hypothesis: A soft mixture-of-experts encoder may beat the current K=128 topk anchor by giving the encoder more routing expressivity without the hard-capacity bottlenecks of grouped routing. Test the existing mixture_topk family at the same K=128 and expansion factor as a quality-first proxy comparison.
next_steps: If mixture_topk shows even modest proxy FVU gain, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before judging promotion.
If mixture_topk is flat or worse under normal runtime, try another expressive family such as residual_topk or factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor.
